Name: SonarWatch Deep Dive
Description:
About the Bounty

SonarWatch is a dashboard which allows you to track all your assets/positions within the DeFi of Solana. In this article you will explore the environment and context in which a dashboard evolve and what are the main areas a dashboard should be focused on.

You will try to answer to the following question :

What role does a dashboard play within one or more ecosystems ?

The article will cover the importance of the dashboard within an ecosystem, how each dashboard competes against each other on a specific blockchain but also with multi-chain dashboards. How they can work together and which benefit can come out of collaboration between them.

The dive will also cover what drives the user from one dashboard to another, explore the key points that make users move between products when it comes to track their portfolio and which features could be the “Killers features” in the race of massive adoption.

Lastly, you will explore how a dashboard can be profitable over the years (you can cover some existing examples) and how you see this side of the product being relevant (or not) for the success of the product.
Mission:
Become an expert on SonarWatch and summarize your findings in a written essay.  

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $1000 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media

🥈 Second Prize

  • $250 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)

🥉 Third Prize

  • $100 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you explore the background of the project?
  • Does your essay talk about the key features of the project?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with this project?
  • Did you learn everything there is to know about the project?
  • Did you take screenshots, provide a step by step guide to onboarding, and clearly articulate the value the project offers?
  • Did you join the project’s Discord and get a feel for the community?
  • Did you try the competition and explain relative pros/cons of this project?

Writing Style

  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?

Submission Requirements

  • Did you write your essay in English?
  • Is your submission at least 1,000 words? Note that typical bounty winners are ~2,000 words.
  • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Did you include links to your sources and references?

Name: SonarWatch Deep Dive
Description:
About the Bounty

SonarWatch is a dashboard which allows you to track all your assets/positions within the DeFi of Solana. In this article you will explore the environment and context in which a dashboard evolve and what are the main areas a dashboard should be focused on.

You will try to answer to the following question :

What role does a dashboard play within one or more ecosystems ?

The article will cover the importance of the dashboard within an ecosystem, how each dashboard competes against each other on a specific blockchain but also with multi-chain dashboards. How they can work together and which benefit can come out of collaboration between them.

The dive will also cover what drives the user from one dashboard to another, explore the key points that make users move between products when it comes to track their portfolio and which features could be the “Killers features” in the race of massive adoption.

Lastly, you will explore how a dashboard can be profitable over the years (you can cover some existing examples) and how you see this side of the product being relevant (or not) for the success of the product.

Become an expert on SonarWatch and summarize your findings in a written essay.

Name: SonarWatch UI/UX Review
Description:
About the Bounty

SonarWatch is a dashboard which allows you to track all your assets/positions within the DeFi of Solana.

A dashboard is basically the front page of the DeFi on a blockchain (when it’s focused on one chain). Therefore it is of great importance that the journey and experience within the tool is as perfect as possible.

For this you need to cover needs from various types of users, newbies, average users, advanced users, corporations, etc..

In this review you will cover the following page of SonarWatch platform :

  • https://sonar.watch/tools and all sub-pages
  • This page regroup small tools that SonarWatch propose to the users to help him through his DeFi experience :
  • Serum DEX Manager gives you the possibility to close your open orders, settle your funds and close your Serum DEX accounts.
  • IL Calculator which is an impermanent loss calculator for all liquidity providers who want to calculate their performance.
  • Unwrap wSOL which lets you unwrap your wSOL with a simple tool.
  • Stake SOL which uses Marinade protocol to stake SOL in exchange of mSOL they can use on DeFi.
  • https://sonar.watch/address-book
  • This page allows you to save all you preferred addresses and also group your main addresses in the “Multi-Wallet”, you can use them on dashboard and NFTs with the selector on the upper left corner of the platform.

Requirement

  • It is best to have a base knowledge on these different tools, to understand which problem they cover to be able to suggest improvement on the UI/UX.
  • The review will be inspired from Vuetify components library (based on Material Design) as the current platform is entirely based on this framework.
  • The review will be in dark theme
Mission:
Conduct a thorough UI/UX review for SonarWatch that matches this example in quality.

You're welcome to complete this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $2000 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media

🥈 Second Prize

  • $500 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)

🥉 Third Prize

  • $250 USDC
  • PRO Status on SonarWatch (Worth 50 SONAR)
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you identify problems for each screen you examined?
  • Did you explain what the user goals are for the different areas of the product you're reviewing?
  • Did you include screenshots of the existing designs?
  • Did you create a new design that solves the problems you identified?
  • Did you redesign the marketing website as well as any application or interior pages? In other words, did you redesign everything that is publicly visible?
  • Did you package your review into a nice presentation (e.g. Notion, Google Slides, Figma, etc)?

Check out previous bounty winners to get a sense of the quality we expect.

Name: Squads Deep Dive
Description:
Squads is a protocol that helps web3-native teams organize, make decisions on-chain and manage digital assets together. Squads make it easy to codify membership, form multi-signature wallets (multi-sigs) called “Vaults,” or use tokenless, on-chain voting for governance.

Rather than issuing a dedicated DAO token, voting power can be customized and assigned to different members of the Squad, giving members more control over how on-chain decision making happens in a light-weight, more efficient way. Squads were designed for decentralized teams building startups, contributing to protocols or co-investing together.
Mission:
Become an expert on Squads and summarize your findings in a written essay.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of two. Bounties will be split among team members.

Name: Stader Explainer Content Bounty
Description:
In Proof-of-Stake blockchains, it’s useful for the ecosystem to have as many stakers as possible. This preserve the decentralisation of the network and creates a more robust ecosystem.

Stader is on a mission to decentralize PoS networks through staking infrastructure that makes it easy for delegators to conveniently and safely stake their tokens. They have outlined their vision for Stader, in their litepaper, as a multi-chain, multi-product platform for staking. Their ultimate goal is to get 1 billion stakers using not only their product suite, but also leveraging third party applications built on top of their infrastructure.

Stader is sponsoring a bounty to help more people understand their platform. The bounty has three prizes for 3 different content types:

  1. Write a Deep Dive Essay to create a one-stop resource for new users to understand everything there is to know about Stader.
  2. Create a Meme Series (or a Video Meme) that can be shared on Twitter to explain Stader’s value quickly and creatively.
  3. Produce a Script for a Video Commercial that could be used in Stader’s marketing. The more imaginative, the better!
Mission: Create clever content that explains how the various Stader products work and how they’re solving for decentralization on PoS networks. You're welcome to work on this bounty individually or in small teams. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize - Best Deep Dive Essay:

  • $1,000 USDC
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
  • An opportunity to join the Stader team full-time

🥈 Second Prize - Best Series of Memes:

  • $300 USDC
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media

🥉 Third Prize - Best Video Script:

  • $200 USDC
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO

‍
Evaluation Criteria:
Deep Dive Essay

  • Did you follow Superteam’s guidelines on how to write a great deep dive essay?
  • Did you explain the Stader product suite in a fun and engaging way that people new to Crypto could understand?
  • Did you include screenshots and instructions on how a new user could use Stader?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with this project?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?

Meme Series

  • Did you create a series of 10+ memes (or a video meme) that explains the Stader product suite and how they solve for decentralisation on PoS networks?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  
  • Did you post your memes on Twitter and tag both @Stader_sol and @SuperteamDAO?
  • Would your meme series resonate with crypto twitter?
  • Is your meme series informative, as well as funny?

Video Script

  • Did you write a script for a 30-60 second video commercial about Stader and their services?
  • Is your script funny, memorable, or otherwise unique in a way that will help Stader stand out?
  • Does your script convey the core value proposition for Stader’s product suite?
  • Is the script written for a global audience?

Submission Requirements

  • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Is your submission in English?

Name: Stader Explainer Content Bounty
Description:
In Proof-of-Stake blockchains, it’s useful for the ecosystem to have as many stakers as possible. This preserve the decentralisation of the network and creates a more robust ecosystem.

Stader is on a mission to decentralize PoS networks through staking infrastructure that makes it easy for delegators to conveniently and safely stake their tokens. They have outlined their vision for Stader, in their litepaper, as a multi-chain, multi-product platform for staking. Their ultimate goal is to get 1 billion stakers using not only their product suite, but also leveraging third party applications built on top of their infrastructure.

Stader is sponsoring a bounty to help more people understand their platform. The bounty has three prizes for 3 different content types:

  1. Write a Deep Dive Essay to create a one-stop resource for new users to understand everything there is to know about Stader.
  2. Create a Meme Series (or a Video Meme) that can be shared on Twitter to explain Stader’s value quickly and creatively.
  3. Produce a Script for a Video Commercial that could be used in Stader’s marketing. The more imaginative, the better!
Mission: Create clever content that explains how the various Stader products work and how they’re solving for decentralization on PoS networks. You're welcome to work on this bounty individually or in small teams. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize - Best Deep Dive Essay:

  • $1,000 USDC
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
  • An opportunity to join the Stader team full-time

🥈 Second Prize - Best Series of Memes:

  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media

  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO

Name: State of Consumer Apps on Solana Deep Dive
Description:
We believe in the Solana ecosystem's potential to onboard the next billion crypto users. It's speed and low transaction fees are ideally suited to consumer applications. We believe an overview of the consumer apps on Solana will be an indispensable resource for the greater community and are excited to offer a bounty to create this public good.

This bounty is sponsored by Solrazr, the first decentralised developer ecosystem for Solana. If you're building on Solana, their platform offers everything you need to get to market.
Mission: Become an expert on the state of consumer apps on Solana.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $500 USD worth of $SOLR
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you learn everything there is to know about the consumer apps on Solana?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed?
  • Did you use a framework to classify the consumer projects?
  • Did you create graphics (low-fidelity is fine) to map our the state of consumer apps?
  • Were you comprehensive in your research?
  • Did you identify projects that have already launched, as well as projects that are launching in the near future?

Writing Style

  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?

Name: State of DAO Tooling on Solana Deep Dive
Description:
In a multi-chain future, essential elements of DAO tooling will be needed on every chain.

We believe in the Solana ecosystem's potential to onboard the next billion crypto users. As a result, DAO infrastructure on Solana will be critical. We believe an overview of the DAO tooling on Solana will be an indispensable resource for the greater community and are excited to offer a bounty to create this common good.
Mission: Become an expert on the state of DAO tooling on Solana.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $500 USDC
  • Instant promotion to "Member Level 2" within the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication within the SuperteamDAO newsletter and on Twitter
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you create/borrow a simple framework to understand the types of tools that are needed to create a DAO?
  • Did you look at the state of DAO tooling on Ethereum (e.g. Daohaus, Collab.land, Coordinape, Snapshot, etc)? Did you find alternatives on Solana?
  • Did you point out the opportunities for new tools to be built/gaps in the existing tech stack?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people interested in DAO Tooling on Solana?
  • Did you learn everything there is to know about DAO tooling on Solana?

Name: State of Decentralized Social Graphs and Creator Economy Projects Deep Dive
Description:
To be truly sovereign in the Information age as a creator means to have complete ownership over your content, its distribution and its monetization. Web3 allows us to move from the creator economy of web2 and power it up to the incoming Ownership Economy of web3. A world in which Content Creators can make content with complete sovereignty and own every single piece of the distribution and monetisation without having to pay the platform or any other corporation.

The goal of this bounty is to explore how far we have gotten in enabling a world which runs on the Ownership Economy. It is a deep dive into the current landscape of all the projects in web3 that are solving for decentralisation of content creation and distribution whether it is in written, audio, or video format as well as providing the tools for it.

This bounty is sponsored by Wordcel, a Decentralised Publishing Stack on Solana that aims to provide content creators as well as projects the freedom to create and own their long-form written content and distribution amongst their readers.
Mission:
Become an expert on the state of Decentralised Social Graph Projects and Tools in web3 and summarize your findings in a written essay.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $1000 USDC
  • Invite to Wordcel Beta
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO

🥈 Second Prize

  • $300 USDC

🥉 Third Prize

  • $200 USDC
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you learn everything there is to learn about the projects currently building decentralised social graphs and content creation tools?
  • Were you comprehensive in your research?
  • Did you convey the need for decentralised creation and ownership of content?
  • Did you deeply explain the projects you chose to understand and how they worked?
  • Did you understand the problems that still exist (if any) with the projects in the ecosystem?
  • Did you explain how Wordcel could have the potential to enter the market and help the mission of giving sovereignty to creators?

Writing Style

  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  

Submission Requirements

  • Did you write your essay in English?
  • Are your submission at least 1,000 words? Note that typical bounty winners are ~2,000 words.
  • Did you submit it before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Did you include links to your sources and references?

Check out previous bounty winners to get a sense of the quality we expect.
Resources:
• Breakpoint 2021 Talk by Balaji Srinivasan
  • What is Wordcel?
  • Variant Fund - The Ownership Economy: Crypto & The Next Frontier of Consumer Software
  • Variant Fund - The Ownership Economy 2022
  • Naval on why Decentralisation matters
  • Chris Dixon - Why Decentralisation matters
  • Problems with social media and their recommendation algorithms
  • ⭐ Superteam’s guidelines on how to write a great deep dive essay

Name: Streamflow Deep Dive
Description:
Streamflow is a suite of products that enables organizations (and individuals) to distribute funds in a simple and straightforward manner. Streamflow offers a powerful and secure multisig wallet for teams to manage their payments and vesting with confidence including a clear summary of their open streams ,signers and more.

The Token vesting & Streaming payments features are fully fledged, allowing the stream creator to cancel the stream prior to completion, or to transfer the stream to another address. For a guided walkthrough to set up your first token vesting stream, check out their guide.

Become an expert on Streamflow and summarize your findings in a written essay.

Name: Switchboard Deep Dive
Description:
Switchboard is a decentralized oracle network that empowers developers to own and configure their own data feeds. Web3 developers rely on real-world data in their applications but blockchains have no way to pull data from external sources. Oracles solve this problem by residing between a blockchain and the internet and facilitate the flow of information for developers to consume downstream.

Most people associate oracles with a price feed but Switchboard took a more generic approach; Switchboard can route any on or off chain data source such as prices, sport results, weather information, or the number of followers Elon Musk has on twitter. Switchboard believes this generic approach to sourcing data will open the door to new web3 applications that were previously out of reach.

The internet is a vast place and sourcing public data can be challenging and time consuming. The Switchboard publisher aims to solve this problem by building a decentralized data catalog where users can contribute job definitions and receive a portion of the protocol fees if a developer builds and funds an on-chain feed (“Democratizing Data”).

Switchboard is looking to the SuperteamDAO to perform a deep dive on Switchboard data feeds, explain the various data feed configuration parameters, and provide a general walkthrough of the publisher. By the end, a reader should have a fundamental understanding of Switchboard data feeds and have the ability to publish their own custom data feed.
Mission:
Become an expert on Switchboard and summarize your findings in a written essay.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.

Name: Timeswap Deep Dive
Description:
Timeswap is a fixed income lending and borrowing protocol for ERC20 tokens. It is a non-custodial, immutable, and censorship resistant protocol leveraging the value of network effects. The maturity time of deposits and loans can be set at any time, giving users astounding flexibility to make money markets that fit the time preferences of most people.

It also allows lenders, borrowers & liquidity providers to freely choose their own risk profiles and set interest rates and collateral ratio for each transaction. Allowing users to determine their own risk appetite.

Timeswap is sponsoring a deep dive bounty for their platform. The winning submission will talk about its various features and provide a detailed breakdown for anybody that wants to get started.
Mission: Become an expert on Timeswap and summarize your findings in a written essay. You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.

Name: UX/UI Redesign of Port Finance
Description:
Web3 is very different than Web2. But the one thing that hasn't changed is the importance of stellar UX/UI. If anything, UX/UI is all the more important given the novelty of the underlying technology.

Port Finance has already, in the short time since its launch, attracted widespread attention for its innovative suite of money market products on Solana. Their lending protocol offers a range of fixed income products with many more on the way.

They're sponsoring a bounty for a talented UX/UI designer to improve their platform and make it easier to use.
Mission: Conduct a thorough UI/UX review for Port Finance that matches this example in quality. You're welcome to complete this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $3,000 worth of PORT tokens
  • Instant access to the SuperteamDAO as a Member
Evaluation Criteria:
Requirements

  • Did you identify problems for each screen you examined?
  • Did you explain what the user goals are for the different areas of the product you're reviewing?
  • Did you include screenshots of the existing designs?
  • Did you create a new design that solves the problems you identified?
  • Did you redesign the marketing website as well as the application itself?
  • Did you package your review into a nice presentation (e.g. Notion, Google Slides, Figma, etc)?

SolRazr’s mission is to help developers raise capital and build fast on Solana. Their platform offers one of the best IDO launchpads around. We at Superteam believe they’re an important part of the future of development on Solana.

SolRazr is sponsoring a bounty for a talented UI/UX designer to improve their digital presence and make it easier to use. The winning submission will provide not only a beautiful and functional design, but also clear reasoning for each design decision that was made.

Name: UXD Stablecoin Deep Dive
Description:
Stablecoins are a critical piece of the crypto ecosystem. They provide the ability for traders, users, and projects to reduce market exposure without needing to completely withdraw their liquidity to a traditional bank account. Unfortunately, most of the prominent stablecoins - including USDT and USDC - are centralized.

UXD is a new project on Solana that aims to create a decentralized stablecoin. With backing from Multicoin, Alameda, and many others, they’re aiming to become the stablecoin of choice in the Solanaverse.

UXD is sponsoring a bounty to help users - both those new to crypto and those new to Solana - better understand how UXD works and why it’s useful. The winning submission will put UXD in context, show how to use their platform, and adhere to our deep dive essay guidelines.
Mission:
Become an expert on UXD and summarize your findings in a written essay.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $1,000 UXD
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media

🥈 Second Prize

  • $250 UXD

🥉 Third Prize

  • $100 UXD
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you discuss the concept of stablecoins and their general value to a crypto ecosystem?
  • Did you compare/contrast UXD with other stablecoins on Solana?
  • Did you assess the value of decentralized stablecoins?
  • Did you discuss how UXD works and how its peg is maintained?
  • Did you include screenshots and instructions demonstrating how to use the project to mint and redeem UXD?
  • Does your essay talk about the key features of the project and it’s upcoming roadmap?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with this project?

Writing Style

  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  
  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?

Submission Requirements

  • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Did you write your essay in English?
  • Is your submission at least 1,000 words? Note that typical bounty winners are ~2,000 words.
  • Did you include links to your sources and references?

Check out previous bounty winners to get a sense of the quality we expect.
Resources:
• Superteam’s guidelines on how to write a great deep dive essay
  • UXD Website
  • UXD Twitter
  • UXD Medium
  • UXD Basics Part 1
  • UXD Basics Part 2
  • What are Stablecoins by Cryptopedia

For any questions, [DM @Abbasshaikh42] (http://(https://twitter.com/Abbasshaikh42))on Twitter or @abbas in our Discord

We're partnering with them to offer a bounty for a written review of the platform and all its features. Any comparison of competitive products is useful as well.
Mission: Use the Grape Network (and its closest competitors), max out all their features, learn everything you can about it, then summarize your findings in a written review.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • 5,000 GRAPE Tokens
  • Published in the Superteam newsletter
  • Published on SuperteamDAO’s Twitter
  • Free access to the SuperteamDAO

🥈 Second Prize

  • 1,000 GRAPE Tokens
  • Free access to the SuperteamDAO

🥉 Third Prize

  • 1,000 GRAPE Tokens
  • Free access to the SuperteamDAO
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you learn everything there is to know about Grape?
  • Did you take screenshots, provide a step by step guide to onboarding, and learn everything there is to know about Grape?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with Grape?
  • Did you join their Discord and get a feel for the community?
  • Did you try the competitor's product?

Writing Style

  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  

[Make sure to check out a previous Bounty winner for an example] (https://superpumped.substack.com/p/symmetryfi-your-crypto-portfolio)of what it takes to take first prize.
Resources:
• The Grape Network website
  • Lightpaper
  • Roadmap
  • Twitter
  • Discord

For any questions, DM @kashdhanda on Twitter
Terms and Conditions: Participation in this bounty is entirely voluntary. Bounties are a way to learn and dabble in opportunities to build in web3. These are neither full time jobs nor project based engagement. Please be advised that the sponsors will not have time for individualised feedback due to the number of entries we receive.

Name: Working in Web3
Description:
Web3 has enabled people to participate in a global permissionless financial internet. Your location, age, sex, race etc are rendered irrelevant in this new paradigm — all that matters is your work and reputation which you can also accrete under the identity of cat or a wassie. This has allowed many to choose to work remotely from smaller towns, picturesque locations and in countries with a lower cost of living. Remote work has always existed — that you can do this now without comprising your ability to earn a competitive wage or ownership (via tokens) is new. Web3 visa is the new H1B visa. Over time, this has the potential to make our world more sustainable by reducing the stress we put on our mega cities, while upgrading our own lifestyles and social bonds lost over the years.

For those adventurers who have moved early into this trend, Superteam is sponsoring a deep dive (with pictures) to share with us a day in your life for those curious about what it is like to work in web3.
Mission: Write a deep dive about your daily routine as a professional working in web3 and share insights that’ll help readers get a glimpse of your life.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize

  • $1000 USDC
  • Membership in the SuperteamDAO
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you explain what you do for work?
  • Did you include pictures and explanations? (Work setup, surroundings, social life etc.)
  • Did you talk about the pros & cons of working remotely?
  • What do you miss about the city life? What do you not miss?
  • To the extent possible, discuss the main lifestyle and financial upsides/downsides to living in a remote location. (Eg., Do you save more/less? Are you more/less stressed?)

Writing Style

  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  

Submission Requirements

  • Did you write your essay in English?
  • No word limit. Be as brief or eloquent as you need to be.
  • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Did you include links to your sources and references?

Check out previous bounty winners to get a sense of the quality we expect.
Resources:
Superteam’s guidelines on how to write a great deep dive essay

For any questions, [DM @Abbasshaikh42] (http://(https://twitter.com/Abbasshaikh42))on Twitter or @abbas in our Discord

Name: Wormhole Deep Dive Bounty
Description:
Wormhole is a decentralized, universal message-passing protocol that connects to multiple blockchains. In simple terms Wormhole allows different blockchains to communicate with each other. It addresses two of the main problems that plague blockchains today:

​

  • Tokens are hard to move between blockchains without relying on centralized exchanges for bridging and swapping tokens. However, this means users are subject to counterparty risk.
  • Decentralized applications (DApps) and smart contracts on different blockchains cannot communicate with each other, making interoperability between chains difficult to achieve.

​

Wormhole solves this by monitoring several chains for messages emitted by smart contracts on those chains. This is achieved through the Wormhole Core Layer & enables cross-chain communication protocols like token and NFT bridges, cross-chain oracles and other messaging applications that would not operate without Wormhole's infrastructure.
Mission:
Become an expert on Wormhole and summarise your findings in a written essay . 

​

Highlight projects that are using wormhole today & explain the impact of cross-chain capabilities on their product/protocol. Include ideas of existing projects or potential use cases that can be improved with wormhole’s suite of cross-chain capabilities. Summarise the competitive landscape including threat & opportunities for wormhole.

​

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of 2. Bounties will be split among team members.
Rewards:
🥇 First Prize: 

  • $1000 USDC
  • Syndication in our newsletter and social media
  • You’ll be added as a Contributor in Superteam

​

🥈 Second Prize

  • $300 USDC

​

🥉 Third Prize

  • $200 USDC

​
Evaluation Criteria:
• Depth
    • Did you explore the background of the project?
    • Does your essay talk about the key features of the project? 
    • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with this project?
    • Did you learn everything there is to know about the project? 
    • Did you take screenshots, provide a step by step guide to onboarding, and clearly articulate the value the project offers?
    • Did you join the project’s Discord and get a feel for the community? 
    • Did you try the competition and explain relative pros/cons of this project? 
  • Writing Style
    • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion? 
    • Is your writing easy and fun to read? 
    • Did you avoid overly complex jargon? 
    • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  
  • Submission Requirements
    • Did you write your essay in English? 
    • Is your submission at least 1,000 words? Note that typical bounty winners are ~2,000 words. 
    • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions. 
    • Did you include links to your sources under the title “References”? If you have used external sources and no references are included, your bounty could be rejected on grounds of plagiarism.
    • Kindly test your submission for plagiarism. If the submission contains more than 5% plagiarised content, your submission will be rejected. Regular offenders can also be disqualified from participating in the bounty program going forward.
    • Share your submission on Twitter & tag @superteamdao + @Wormholecrypto & use the hashtag #Superteambounty and submit this link in your bounty application.

Name: Zeta Markets Deep Dive
Description:
Zeta is the premier under-collateralized DeFi futures & options DEX built on Solana. Zeta is unlocking the power of derivatives trading on a DEX by bringing CeFi derivatives trading capabilities to DeFi. They currently offer dated futures & options on $SOL.

Zeta has numerous unique features notably under-collateralized trading (unlocking leverage & capital efficiency) & recently released new order types (Limit, Post-only, Fill-or-kill) - giving traders lots of flexibility in executing their strategies. All of this is packaged in a beautiful UX but also available programmatically via their open-source SDK.
Mission:
Become an expert on Zeta’s trading experience either via the UX or programmatically via the SDK (bonus points for both!) and summarize your trading experience in an easy-to-understand fashion.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of two. Bounties will be split among team members.

Zeta is the premier under-collateralized DeFi futures & options DEX built on Solana. Zeta is unlocking the power of derivatives trading on a DEX by bringing CeFi derivatives trading capabilities to DeFi. They currently offer dated futures & options on $SOL.

Zeta has numerous unique features notably under-collateralized trading (unlocking leverage & capital efficiency) & recently released new order types (Limit, Post-only, Fill-or-kill) - giving traders lots of flexibility in executing their strategies. All of this is packaged in a beautiful UX but also available programmatically via their open-source SDK.
Mission:
Become an expert on Zeta’s trading experience either via the UX or programmatically via the SDK (bonus points for both!) and summarize your trading experience in an easy-to-understand fashion.

You're welcome to write this review individually or in teams of two. Bounties will be split among team members.

Evaluation Criteria:
Depth

  • Did you explore the background of the project?
  • Does your essay talk about the key features of the project?
  • Is your essay the one-stop-resource for people to get up to speed with this project?
  • Did you learn everything there is to know about the project?
  • Did you take screenshots, provide a step by step guide to onboarding, and clearly articulate the value the project offers?
  • Did you join the project’s Discord and get a feel for the community?
  • Did you try the competition and explain relative pros/cons of this project?

Writing Style

  • Did you write a compelling introduction and a satisfying conclusion?
  • Is your writing easy and fun to read?
  • Did you avoid overly complex jargon?
  • Did you explain concepts in a way a person new to crypto could understand?  

Submission Requirements

  • Did you write your essay in English?
  • Is your submission at least 1,000 words? Note that typical bounty winners are ~2,000 words.
  • Did you submit before the deadline? Note: there will be no deadline extensions.
  • Did you include links to your sources and references?

Check out previous bounty winners to get a sense of the quality we expect.
